subject: chemistry
description: NGSS HS Chemistry: Structure & Properties of Matter, Chemical Reactions, Stoichiometry, Thermochemistry, Kinetics & Equilibrium.

domains.name: 原子与键 / Atoms & Bonding / Atoms & Bonding

nodes.name: 原子结构
curriculum_points: HS-PS1-1: Use the periodic table as a model to predict the relative properties of elements based on the patterns of electrons in the outermost energy level of atoms.
HS-PS1-8: Develop models to illustrate the changes in the composition of the nucleus of the atom and the energy released during the processes of fission, fusion, and radioactive decay.

nodes.name: 周期性趋势
curriculum_points: HS-PS1-1: Use the periodic table as a model to predict the relative properties of elements based on the patterns of electrons in the outermost energy level of atoms.

nodes.name: 化学键
curriculum_points: HS-PS1-1: Use the periodic table as a model to predict the relative properties of elements based on the patterns of electrons in the outermost energy level of atoms.

HS-PS1-2: Construct and revise an explanation for the outcome of a simple chemical reaction based on the outermost electron states of atoms, trends in the periodic table, and knowledge of the patterns of chemical properties.

nodes.name: 分子几何 VSEPR
curriculum_points: HS-PS1-1: Use the periodic table as a model to predict the relative properties of elements based on the patterns of electrons in the outermost energy level of atoms.

HS-PS1-2: Construct and revise an explanation for the outcome of a simple chemical reaction based on the outermost electron states of atoms, trends in the periodic table, and knowledge of the patterns of chemical properties.
HS-PS1-3: Plan and conduct an investigation to gather evidence to compare the structure of substances at the bulk scale to infer the strength of electrical forces between particles.

nodes.name: 分子间作用力
curriculum_points: HS-PS1-3: Plan and conduct an investigation to gather evidence to compare the structure of substances at the bulk scale to infer the strength of electrical forces between particles.

domains.name: 反应与化学计量 / Reactions & Stoichiometry / Reactions & Stoichiometry

nodes.name: 反应类型
curriculum_points: HS-PS1-2: Construct and revise an explanation for the outcome of a simple chemical reaction based on the outermost electron states of atoms, trends in the periodic table, and knowledge of the patterns of chemical properties.

nodes.name: 配平方程
curriculum_points: HS-PS1-7: Use mathematical representations to support the claim that atoms, and therefore mass, are conserved during a chemical reaction.

nodes.name: 摩尔
curriculum_points: HS-PS1-7: Use mathematical representations to support the claim that atoms, and therefore mass, are conserved during a chemical reaction.

domains.name: 热力学与动力学 / Thermo & Kinetics / Thermo & Kinetics

nodes.name: 热化学
curriculum_points: HS-PS1-4: Develop a model to illustrate that the release or absorption of energy from a chemical reaction system depends on the changes in total bond energy.

nodes.name: 反应速率
curriculum_points: HS-PS1-5: Apply scientific principles and evidence to provide an explanation about the effects of changing the temperature or concentration of the reacting particles on the rate at which a reaction occurs.

nodes.name: 化学平衡
curriculum_points: HS-PS1-6: Refine the design of a chemical system by specifying a change in conditions that would produce increased amounts of products at equilibrium.

nodes.name: 勒沙特列原理
curriculum_points: HS-PS1-6: Refine the design of a chemical system by specifying a change in conditions that would produce increased amounts of products at equilibrium.

nodes.name: 酸碱理论

domains.name: 气体与溶液 / Gases & Solutions / Gases & Solutions

nodes.name: 气体定律
curriculum_points: HS-PS1-3: Plan and conduct an investigation to gather evidence to compare the structure of substances at the bulk scale to infer the strength of electrical forces between particles.

HS-PS3-2: Develop and use models to illustrate that energy at the macroscopic scale can be accounted for as a combination of energy associated with the motions of particles (objects) and energy associated with the relative position of particles (objects).
